info.name: Realtime Chat API
info.description:
# Realtime Chat API — Complete Postman Collection

## Setup Instructions

1. **Import** this collection into Postman
2. **Set Environment Variables** (create a new environment):
   - `baseUrl` → `http://localhost:3000/api/v1`
   - All other variables (`accessToken`, `refreshToken`, etc.) are **auto-populated** by pre/post-request scripts

3. **Run in order**:
   - Start with `Auth → Register` or `Auth → Login`
   - Tokens are stored automatically in collection variables
   - Subsequent requests use those tokens automatically

## Test Credentials (after seeding)
- `alice@example.com` / `Password123!` (ADMIN)
- `bob@example.com` / `Password123!` (USER)
- `charlie@example.com` / `Password123!` (USER)

## WebSocket Testing
See the **WebSocket** folder — it contains a pre-built Socket.IO client script you can run in the Postman WebSocket tab.

variable.description: Base API URL. Change port if needed.
Auto-set after creating a group chat

exec: // Auth is set per-collection via the auth variable above
// Global test: verify response is valid JSON

item.name: 🏥 Health
item.description: Liveness and readiness probe endpoints. No authentication required.

request.description: Returns 200 if the Node.js process is alive. Used by Docker/K8s liveness probe.
Returns 200 if DB and Redis are reachable. Returns 503 if any dependency is down. Used by K8s readiness probe.

item.name: 🔐 Auth
item.description:
Authentication endpoints. Register, login, token refresh, logout.

**Token Flow:**
1. Register or Login → saves `accessToken` and `refreshToken` to collection variables
2. All protected requests automatically use `{{accessToken}}`
3. When access token expires → call Refresh Tokens
4. Logout revokes the session

request.description:
Register a new user account.

**Validation rules:**
- `email`: valid email format
- `username`: 3-30 chars, letters/numbers/underscores only
- `displayName`: 2-50 chars
- `password`: min 8 chars, must include uppercase, lowercase, number, symbol

**Rate limit:** 5 requests/minute per IP

Login with email and password.

**Security note:** Returns the same error message for both wrong email AND wrong password to prevent user enumeration.

**Rate limit:** 10 requests/minute per IP

**Seeded test accounts:**
- `alice@example.com` / `Password123!` (ADMIN)
- `bob@example.com` / `Password123!`
- `charlie@example.com` / `Password123!`
Negative test — verifies that wrong credentials return 401 with no information about which field failed (user enumeration protection).

exec: pm.test('Error message does not reveal which field failed', () => {

request.description: Negative test — verifies ValidationPipe rejects malformed input before it reaches the service layer.

Exchange a refresh token for a new access + refresh token pair.

**Token Rotation:** The used refresh token is immediately revoked. A new pair is issued.

**Theft detection:** If a stolen token is used after rotation, all sessions for that user are revoked and they must re-login.

exec: // New tokens should differ from old ones

request.description: Revoke the current session's refresh token. Requires a valid access token.
Revoke ALL refresh tokens for the current user — logs them out of every device/session simultaneously.

exec: // Clear saved tokens since they're now invalid
console.log('All sessions revoked — tokens cleared from collection');

item.name: 👤 Users
item.description:
User profile management. Search, view, and update user profiles.

All endpoints require a valid `Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}` header (set automatically from collection variable).

request.description: Returns the authenticated user's full profile.

Update the authenticated user's profile fields.

**Updatable fields:** `displayName`, `bio`, `avatarUrl`

All fields are optional — only send what you want to change.

query.description: Search term (matches username or displayName, case-insensitive)

request.description:
Search users by username or display name. Case-insensitive partial match.

Useful for: adding members to group chats, starting new DMs.

exec: // Save the first user's ID for use in chat creation

request.description:
Fetch any user's public profile by their UUID.

Returns 404 if user doesn't exist or has been soft-deleted.

Fetch a user's profile by their username (without @).

Useful when users share their username handle.
Negative test — verifies that requesting a non-existent UUID returns 404.
Negative test — verifies that protected routes reject unauthenticated requests.

item.name: 💬 Chats
item.description:
Chat room management — create private DMs, group chats, manage members.

**Flow:**
1. Search for a user → get `recipientId`
2. Create Private Chat → get `chatId`
3. Use `chatId` in Messages requests

Or:
1. Create Group Chat with multiple `memberIds`

request.description:
Start a 1-on-1 DM with another user.

**Idempotent:** If a DM between these two users already exists, it returns the existing chat instead of creating a duplicate.

Requires `recipientId` — run `Search Users` first to populate `{{recipientId}}`.

exec: console.warn('⚠️ recipientId not set. Run "Search Users" first.');

request.description:
Create a group chat with multiple members.

- Creator becomes **OWNER** automatically
- `memberIds` are the initial members (can add more later)
- `name` is required for group chats
- Max 100 members total

Returns all chats the authenticated user is a member of.

**Includes per-chat:**
- `unreadCount`: messages since last read
- `lastMessage`: preview of the most recent message
- `memberCount`: number of active members

Sorted by `updatedAt` descending (most recently active first).

Fetch details of a specific chat.

Returns 403 if the authenticated user is not a member of this chat.

Add one or more users to a group chat.

**Requires:** ADMIN or OWNER role in the chat.

**Idempotent:** Adding an already-existing member is a no-op (skipDuplicates).

Remove a member from a group chat.

- **Self-removal:** Any member can remove themselves (leave)
- **Removing others:** Requires ADMIN or OWNER role
- **Sets `leftAt`** timestamp (soft removal, chat history preserved)
Negative test — verifies that non-members cannot access chats they don't belong to.

item.name: 📨 Messages
item.description:
Message operations within a chat room.

All endpoints are scoped to a chat: `POST /chats/:chatId/messages`

**Prerequisites:** `{{chatId}}` must be set (run Create Private Chat first).

**Pagination:** Messages use cursor-based pagination — pass `cursor` (a messageId) to get messages before that point.

request.description:
Send a text message to a chat.

**Message types:** `TEXT`, `IMAGE`, `FILE`, `SYSTEM`

**Optional fields:**
- `replyToId`: UUID of message being replied to (creates thread)
- `mediaUrl`: URL for IMAGE/FILE messages

Also sends a WebSocket `message:new` event to all chat members.
Send a reply to a specific message by including `replyToId`.

query.description: MessageID cursor — returns messages before this ID (leave empty for latest)

request.description:
Fetch paginated messages for a chat.

**Cursor pagination:** Returns messages newest-first. To load older messages, pass the ID of the oldest message as `cursor`.

**Response includes `meta`:**
- `hasNextPage`: true if older messages exist
- `total`: total message count in chat

Only returns non-deleted messages. Soft-deleted messages show as `content: null`.

Edit a message's content.

**Sender only** — only the message author can edit it.

Sets `editedAt` timestamp so recipients can see it was edited.

Mark one or more messages as read by the current user.

**Effects:**
1. Creates `MessageRead` records for each message
2. Updates `ChatMember.lastReadAt` watermark (resets unread count)
3. Triggers `message:read:ack` WebSocket event to chat members

Add an emoji reaction to a message.

**Idempotent:** Adding the same emoji twice is a no-op (upsert).

Emoji value: any valid Unicode emoji string (👍, ❤️, 😂, 🎉, etc.)
Add a second different reaction to the same message.
Fetch messages to verify reactions appear in the response.

Remove an emoji reaction from a message.

The emoji in the URL path must be URL-encoded: 👍 = `%F0%9F%91%8D`

Soft-delete a message.

- **Sender:** can delete their own messages
- **Admin/Owner:** can delete any message in their chat

**Soft delete behavior:**
- `content` is set to `null`
- `deletedAt` is set
- Read receipts and reactions are preserved
- Message ID still exists (no broken reply chains)
Negative test — verifies that non-members cannot send messages to a chat.

item.name: 🔁 Full Flow Test
item.description:
An automated end-to-end flow that exercises the entire API in sequence.

Run this folder as a **Collection Run** to test the complete happy path:
1. Register user
2. Login
3. Search for another user
4. Create DM
5. Send messages
6. Add reactions
7. Mark as read
8. Logout

exec: pm.test('[Step 6] Messages marked as read (204)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(204));
console.log('\n🎉 Full flow test PASSED! All 7 steps completed successfully.');

item.name: 🔌 WebSocket Reference
item.description:
WebSocket events reference documentation.

Postman supports WebSocket connections natively. To test:
1. Open a new tab → WebSocket Request
2. URL: `ws://localhost:3000/chat`
3. Set handshake headers: `Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}`

Or use the Socket.IO protocol with `socket.io-client` in the Console tab.

**Event flow:**
```
connect → join chat → send messages → typing indicators → disconnect
```

See `docs/websocket-client-guide.ts` in the project for full frontend integration code.

request.description:
# WebSocket Connection

**URL:** `ws://localhost:3000/chat`  
**Protocol:** Socket.IO  
**Namespace:** `/chat`

## Client Connection
```javascript
const socket = io('http://localhost:3000/chat', {
  auth: { token: 'Bearer <accessToken>' }
});
```

## Events to Emit (Client → Server)

| Event | Payload |
|-------|---------|
| `chat:join` | `{ chatId: string }` |
| `chat:leave` | `{ chatId: string }` |
| `message:send` | `{ chatId, content, type?, replyToId? }` |
| `message:edit` | `{ chatId, messageId, content }` |
| `message:delete` | `{ chatId, messageId }` |
| `typing:start` | `{ chatId: string }` |
| `typing:stop` | `{ chatId: string }` |
| `message:read` | `{ chatId, messageIds: string[] }` |
| `reaction:add` | `{ chatId, messageId, emoji }` |
| `reaction:remove` | `{ chatId, messageId, emoji }` |
| `presence:heartbeat` | `{}` (every 20s) |

## Events to Listen (Server → Client)

| Event | Payload |
|-------|---------|
| `message:new` | `MessageDto` |
| `message:updated` | `MessageDto` |
| `message:deleted` | `{ messageId, chatId }` |
| `typing:user` | `{ userId, username, chatId }` |
| `typing:user:stop` | `{ userId, username, chatId }` |
| `presence:online` | `{ userId, status: 'ONLINE' }` |
| `presence:offline` | `{ userId, status: 'OFFLINE' }` |
| `message:read:ack` | `{ userId, messageIds[], readAt }` |
| `reaction:added` | `{ messageId, chatId, emoji, userId }` |
| `reaction:removed` | `{ messageId, chatId, emoji, userId }` |
| `error` | `{ message, errorCode? }` |
